title: [EPIC] Project Foundation & Architecture
body:
## Epic: Project Foundation & Architecture

This epic covers all the scaffolding work needed to establish a production-quality Go project that is buildable, testable, and deployable from day one.

### Child issues

- Set up Go module and project structure
- Create SQLite storage layer with embedded migrations
- Implement YAML configuration system with env var overrides
- Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline
- Create multi-stage Dockerfile and docker-compose
- Write curl install script

### Acceptance criteria

- `make build` produces a binary
- `make test` passes
- `docker compose up` starts the service
- `curl install.sh | sh` installs on a fresh Ubuntu 22.04 VM

title: Set up Go module and project structure
body:
## Summary

Initialise the Go module and establish the standard directory layout for the project.

## Motivation

A consistent layout makes it easy for contributors to navigate the codebase and for tooling to locate files.

## Acceptance criteria

- `go.mod` declares module `github.com/openfiltr/openfiltr` with Go 1.23
- Directories exist: `cmd/server`, `internal/{api,auth,config,dns,filtering,storage}`, `pkg/sdk`
- `make build` compiles a runnable binary
- `make test` runs (even if no tests exist yet)

## Implementation notes

- Use `cmd/server/main.go` as the entrypoint
- Follow standard Go project layout conventions
- Add `.air.toml` for live-reload development

title: Create SQLite storage layer with embedded migrations
body:
## Summary

Implement a database layer using SQLite with WAL mode and an embedded, sequential migration system.

## Motivation

SQLite is ideal for a self-hosted tool — zero external dependencies, single file, easy backup. Embedded migrations mean the binary is fully self-contained.

## Acceptance criteria

- `storage.Open(path)` opens/creates the database with WAL mode enabled
- `storage.Migrate(db)` applies all pending migrations idempotently
- Schema includes tables: `users`, `api_tokens`, `block_rules`, `allow_rules`, `rule_sources`, `dns_entries`, `upstream_servers`, `clients`, `groups`, `activity_log`, `audit_events`, `settings`
- All tables have `id TEXT PRIMARY KEY` (UUID) and `created_at`/`updated_at` timestamps
- Migrations are tracked in a `schema_migrations` table

## Implementation notes

- Use `github.com/mattn/go-sqlite3` with CGO
- `db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)` to avoid WAL writer conflicts
- Enable `_foreign_keys=on` and `_busy_timeout=5000` in the DSN

title: Implement YAML configuration system with env var overrides
body:
## Summary

Build a configuration loader that reads from a YAML file and allows selected values to be overridden by environment variables.

## Motivation

YAML is human-readable and version-controllable. Env var overrides allow secrets (JWT key, DB path) to be injected at runtime without modifying the config file.

## Acceptance criteria

- `config.Load(path)` reads YAML; creates default config if file is missing
- `OPENFILTR_JWT_SECRET` overrides `auth.jwt_secret`
- `OPENFILTR_DB_PATH` overrides `storage.path`
- Config struct covers: `server.listen_http`, `server.listen_dns`, `dns.upstream_servers`, `storage.path`, `auth.jwt_secret`, `auth.token_expiry_hours`
- Default config is written to disk on first run

## Implementation notes

- Use `gopkg.in/yaml.v3`
- Never log the JWT secret

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline (test, build, lint)
body:
## Summary

Create a CI workflow that runs tests, builds binaries for multiple architectures, builds the frontend, and runs security checks on every PR.

## Motivation

Automated CI ensures code quality and prevents regressions on every contribution.

## Acceptance criteria

- Workflow triggers on `push` to `main`/`develop` and on all PRs to `main`
- Jobs: backend tests with race detector, binary build for linux/amd64 and linux/arm64, frontend build, Docker build
- Failing tests block PR merge
- Build artefacts are uploaded
- CodeQL security analysis runs on `main` pushes and weekly

## Implementation notes

- Use `actions/setup-go@v5` with module cache enabled
- `sudo apt-get install gcc libsqlite3-dev` for CGO
- Use `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` for release automation

title: Create multi-stage Dockerfile and docker-compose
body:
## Summary

Create a production-ready multi-stage Dockerfile and a docker-compose file for easy deployment.

## Motivation

Docker is the most common deployment method for self-hosted tools. A multi-stage build keeps the final image small and secure.

## Acceptance criteria

- Stage 1 (node:20-alpine): builds the React frontend
- Stage 2 (golang:1.23-alpine): builds the Go binary with CGO
- Stage 3 (alpine:3.20): final image with binary, web/dist, openapi/ — non-root user, EXPOSE 3000 5353/udp
- `docker compose up` starts the service and passes the healthcheck
- Image size < 50MB
- `HEALTHCHECK` calls `/api/v1/system/health`

## Implementation notes

- Use `adduser -S -G openfiltr openfiltr` for the runtime user
- Mount `/data` and `/etc/openfiltr` as volumes
- Pass `VERSION` build arg to set `main.version`

title: [EPIC] DNS Filtering Engine
body:
## Epic: DNS Filtering Engine

All work related to the DNS server itself — forwarding, filtering, caching, and local resolution.

### Child issues

- Implement basic UDP DNS forwarding server
- Implement block rule matching (exact, wildcard, regex)
- Implement allow rule matching and precedence
- Implement local DNS entry resolution (A, AAAA, CNAME)
- Add DNS response caching with configurable TTL
- Implement per-client DNS policy lookup
- Add DNSSEC validation support (v1.1)
- Add DoH upstream support (v1.1)
- Add DoT upstream support (v1.1)

### Acceptance criteria

- DNS queries are resolved within 50ms on average
- Blocked domains return NXDOMAIN
- Local entries override upstream
- Allow rules take precedence over block rules

title: Implement basic UDP DNS forwarding server
body:
## Summary

Build a UDP DNS server that forwards queries to configurable upstream resolvers.

## Motivation

DNS forwarding is the core function of OpenFiltr. All filtering and local resolution is layered on top.

## Acceptance criteria

- Listens on a configurable UDP address (default `:53`)
- Forwards all queries to the first available upstream server
- Falls back to the next upstream on timeout or error
- Writes resolution latency to `activity_log`
- Handles graceful shutdown

## Implementation notes

- Use `github.com/miekg/dns`
- Set `Timeout: 5*time.Second` on the upstream client
- Use `dns.NewServeMux()` for handler routing

title: Implement block rule matching (exact, wildcard, regex)
body:
## Summary

Query the `block_rules` table on every DNS request and return NXDOMAIN for matches.

## Motivation

Block rules are the primary filtering mechanism.

## Acceptance criteria

- `rule_type=exact`: `example.com` matches only `example.com`
- `rule_type=wildcard`: `*.example.com` matches `foo.example.com` and `a.b.example.com` but not `example.com`
- `rule_type=regex`: pattern is compiled and matched against the full domain
- Only `enabled=1` rules are evaluated
- Matching is case-insensitive
- SQL query uses indexed columns and completes in < 5ms for 100k rules

## Implementation notes

- Compile regex patterns at startup or cache them
- Use a prepared statement for bulk exact/wildcard lookup

title: Implement allow rule matching and precedence over block rules
body:
## Summary

Implement allow rules that let specific domains bypass block rules.

## Motivation

Block rule lists often have false positives. Allow rules let users whitelist specific domains without modifying the source list.

## Acceptance criteria

- Allow rules are checked before block rules
- A domain matching an allow rule is always forwarded, even if it also matches a block rule
- Same rule types supported as block rules (exact, wildcard, regex)
- Only `enabled=1` rules are evaluated

## Implementation notes

- Resolution order: allow rules → block rules → local entries → upstream

title: Implement local DNS entry resolution (A, AAAA, CNAME)
body:
## Summary

Allow users to define custom DNS records that are served directly without forwarding upstream.

## Motivation

Local DNS entries let users assign friendly names to home network devices (e.g. `router.home → 192.168.1.1`) without running a full authoritative DNS server.

## Acceptance criteria

- Supports entry types: A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT
- Local entries are served before querying upstream
- TTL is configurable per entry (default 300)
- Only `enabled=1` entries are served

## Implementation notes

- Query `dns_entries` table by `host` and `entry_type`
- Return correct `dns.RR` types from `github.com/miekg/dns`

title: Add DNS response caching with configurable TTL
body:
## Summary

Cache upstream DNS responses in memory to reduce latency and upstream query volume.

## Motivation

Many domains are queried repeatedly. Caching reduces latency from ~20ms to <1ms for cached responses.

## Acceptance criteria

- Upstream responses are cached in memory using the DNS TTL
- `dns.cache_ttl` config value caps the maximum cache TTL
- Cache is invalidated when block/allow rules change
- Cache hit rate is visible in `/api/v1/system/stats`

## Implementation notes

- Use a simple `sync.Map` or `github.com/patrickmn/go-cache`
- Key: `{domain}:{qtype}`

title: Implement per-client DNS policy lookup
body:
## Summary

Look up registered clients by source IP on each query and apply group-level filtering policies.

## Motivation

Different devices on a network may need different filtering policies (e.g. stricter rules for children's devices).

## Acceptance criteria

- Client is identified by source IP or CIDR match
- Client's group rules are applied in addition to global rules
- Unregistered clients use the global ruleset
- Client lookups are cached for 60 seconds

## Implementation notes

- Query `clients` table by `identifier` on each request
- Join to `groups` for group-level rules

title: Add DNSSEC validation support
body:
## Summary

Validate DNSSEC signatures on upstream responses to protect against DNS spoofing.

## Motivation

DNSSEC validation provides cryptographic assurance that responses have not been tampered with.

## Acceptance criteria

- DNSSEC validation can be enabled/disabled via config
- Responses that fail DNSSEC validation are rejected and logged
- Validation result is included in the activity log

## Implementation notes

- Use `github.com/miekg/dns` DNSSEC validation utilities
- Set `DO=true` in upstream queries

title: Add DoH (DNS-over-HTTPS) upstream support
body:
## Summary

Allow upstream DNS queries to be sent over HTTPS to DoH-capable resolvers.

## Motivation

DoH prevents upstream DNS queries from being intercepted or modified in transit.

## Acceptance criteria

- `protocol: doh` on an upstream server config uses HTTPS
- Standard DoH wire format (`application/dns-message`)
- Falls back to other upstreams on error
- Works with Cloudflare (`1.1.1.1`) and Quad9 (`9.9.9.9`) DoH endpoints

## Implementation notes

- Use `net/http` with TLS verification enabled
- POST method with `Content-Type: application/dns-message`

title: Add DoT (DNS-over-TLS) upstream support
body:
## Summary

Allow upstream DNS queries to be sent over TLS (port 853) to DoT-capable resolvers.

## Motivation

DoT provides encrypted DNS transport without requiring HTTPS.

## Acceptance criteria

- `protocol: dot` on an upstream server config uses TLS on port 853
- TLS certificate is verified by default; `insecure_skip_verify` option available
- Connection pooling is used to amortise TLS handshake cost

## Implementation notes

- Use `github.com/miekg/dns` with `Net: "tcp-tls"`

title: [EPIC] REST API — All Endpoints
body:
## Epic: REST API

Implement all REST API endpoints documented in the OpenAPI 3.1 specification.

### Scope

Every feature in OpenFiltr must be accessible through the API. The UI is a consumer of the API, not a special case.

### Child issues

- Auth endpoints (login, logout, setup, me)
- API token management
- Block rules CRUD
- Allow rules CRUD
- Rule sources CRUD + refresh
- Upstream servers CRUD
- DNS entries CRUD
- Clients and groups CRUD
- Activity log API
- Config export/import
- Audit log API
- Rate limiting
- Webhook events (v1.1)
- Prometheus metrics (v1.1)

### Acceptance criteria

- All endpoints documented in `openapi/openapi.yaml`
- All list endpoints return `{items:[...], total:N}`
- All error responses return `{error:{message:""}}`
- All write operations are logged to `audit_events`

title: Implement authentication endpoints (login, logout, setup, me)
body:
## Summary

Implement the core authentication flow: first-run setup, login, logout, and current-user profile.

## Acceptance criteria

- `POST /api/v1/auth/setup` — creates first admin user; returns 409 if already set up; requires `username` + `password` (min 8 chars)
- `POST /api/v1/auth/login` — validates credentials, returns JWT, sets `HttpOnly`/`Secure` session cookie
- `POST /api/v1/auth/logout` — clears session cookie
- `GET /api/v1/auth/me` — returns `{id, username, role}` for authenticated user
- Invalid credentials return 401 (no user enumeration)

## Implementation notes

- Use `golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt` with `DefaultCost`
- JWT signed with `HS256`; claims include `user_id`, `username`, `role`
- Cookie: `SameSite=Strict`, `Path=/`, `MaxAge` from config

title: Implement API token management (create, list, delete)
body:
## Summary

Allow users to create named API tokens for programmatic access, scoped to specific operations.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/auth/tokens` — lists tokens for the current user (no raw token values)
- `POST /api/v1/auth/tokens` — creates a new token; returns the raw token **once only**
- `DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/{id}` — revokes a token
- Tokens begin with `oft_` and are 32 random bytes (hex-encoded)
- Token hash is stored in `api_tokens.token_hash` using bcrypt
- Optional `expires_at` (RFC3339)

## Implementation notes

- `last_used_at` is updated on each authenticated request
- Brute-force protection: token validation is O(n) — add index on `user_id`

title: Implement block rules CRUD API
body:
## Summary

CREATE, READ, UPDATE, DELETE endpoints for DNS block rules.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/filtering/block-rules` — paginated (`limit`, `offset`), filterable by `search` (pattern prefix match)
- `POST /api/v1/filtering/block-rules` — requires `pattern`; optional `rule_type` (default `exact`), `comment`, `enabled`
- `GET /api/v1/filtering/block-rules/{id}` — 404 if not found
- `PUT /api/v1/filtering/block-rules/{id}` — partial update
- `DELETE /api/v1/filtering/block-rules/{id}` — 404 if not found
- All writes logged to `audit_events`

## Implementation notes

- ID is a UUID (`github.com/google/uuid`)
- Validate `rule_type` is one of: `exact`, `wildcard`, `regex`
- Reject empty `pattern`

title: Implement allow rules CRUD API
body:
## Summary

CREATE, READ, UPDATE, DELETE endpoints for DNS allow rules.

## Acceptance criteria

- Same shape as block rules API: `GET /api/v1/filtering/allow-rules`, `POST`, `GET /{id}`, `PUT /{id}`, `DELETE /{id}`
- Allow rules take precedence over block rules in the DNS engine
- Validate `rule_type`: `exact`, `wildcard`, `regex`
- All writes logged to `audit_events`

## Implementation notes

- Reuse the same handler pattern as block rules — only the table name differs

title: Implement rule sources CRUD API with manual refresh
body:
## Summary

Manage external domain blocklists (hosts files, domain lists) via the API.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/filtering/sources` — list all sources with `rule_count` and `last_updated_at`
- `POST /api/v1/filtering/sources` — create with `name`, `url`, `format` (`hosts`, `domain`, `adblock`)
- `PUT /api/v1/filtering/sources/{id}` — update
- `DELETE /api/v1/filtering/sources/{id}` — delete
- `POST /api/v1/filtering/sources/{id}/refresh` — fetch the URL and upsert rules into `block_rules`

## Implementation notes

- HTTP fetch with 30s timeout
- Parse hosts format: skip `#` comments, skip `127.0.0.1`/`0.0.0.0` prefix
- Update `rule_count` and `last_updated_at` after successful fetch

title: Implement upstream servers CRUD API
body:
## Summary

Manage DNS upstream server configuration through the API.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/dns/upstream-servers` — list all upstream servers
- `POST /api/v1/dns/upstream-servers` — create with `name`, `address`, `protocol` (default `udp`), `priority`
- `GET /api/v1/dns/upstream-servers/{id}` — get single
- `PUT /api/v1/dns/upstream-servers/{id}` — update
- `DELETE /api/v1/dns/upstream-servers/{id}` — delete
- Validate `protocol` is one of: `udp`, `tcp`, `doh`, `dot`
- Changes take effect immediately (no restart required)

## Implementation notes

- DNS server should reload upstream list from DB on each forward attempt

title: Implement DNS entries CRUD API
body:
## Summary

CRUD API for local DNS records (custom hostnames).

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/dns/entries` — paginated list
- `POST /api/v1/dns/entries` — create with `host`, `entry_type` (A/AAAA/CNAME/MX/TXT/PTR/SRV), `value`, `ttl`, `comment`
- `GET /api/v1/dns/entries/{id}` — get single
- `PUT /api/v1/dns/entries/{id}` — update
- `DELETE /api/v1/dns/entries/{id}` — delete
- Validate `entry_type` against allowed values
- Validate `value` format per type (e.g. valid IP for A records)

## Implementation notes

- DNS engine should re-query the DB on each request (or cache with short TTL)

title: Implement clients and groups CRUD API
body:
## Summary

Register network clients and organise them into groups for policy management.

## Acceptance criteria

**Clients**
- `GET /api/v1/clients` — list all clients
- `POST /api/v1/clients` — register with `name`, `identifier`, `identifier_type` (ip/cidr/mac), optional `group_id`
- `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/clients/{id}`

**Groups**
- `GET /api/v1/groups` — list all groups
- `POST /api/v1/groups` — create with `name`, optional `description`
- `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/groups/{id}`

## Implementation notes

- Validate `identifier_type` is one of `ip`, `cidr`, `mac`
- Validate IP/CIDR format using `net.ParseIP` / `net.ParseCIDR`

title: Implement activity log API with filters and pagination
body:
## Summary

Expose the DNS query activity log through a paginated, filterable REST API.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/activity` — paginated (`limit`, `offset`); filterable by `client_ip`, `domain`, `action` (allowed/blocked)
- `GET /api/v1/activity/stats` — returns `{total_queries, blocked_queries, allowed_queries, block_rate, top_blocked_domains:[{domain,count}]}`
- Items are returned newest-first
- No write operations (activity log is append-only)

## Implementation notes

- Add DB index on `created_at`, `client_ip`, `domain` for query performance
- `top_blocked_domains` uses `GROUP BY domain ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 10`

title: Implement YAML config export endpoint
body:
## Summary

Add an API endpoint that exports the current configuration as a YAML file.

## Motivation

Portable YAML export enables backup, version control, and migration between instances.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/config/export` returns `Content-Type: application/yaml`
- Export includes all block rules, allow rules, rule sources, DNS entries, upstream servers
- Export includes a `version` and `exported_at` field
- Sensitive fields (JWT secret, password hashes) are **excluded**

## Implementation notes

- Use `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` for marshalling
- Filename suggestion via `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="openfiltr-config.yaml"`

title: Implement YAML config import with validation
body:
## Summary

Add an API endpoint that imports a YAML configuration, replacing or merging existing data.

## Acceptance criteria

- `POST /api/v1/config/import` accepts `application/yaml` or `application/json`
- Import validates the schema version field
- Supports `dry_run=true` query parameter — validates without writing
- Returns a summary: `{created:N, updated:N, deleted:N, errors:[]}`
- Invalid YAML returns 400 with specific error location

## Implementation notes

- Wrap the entire import in a single DB transaction
- Rollback on any validation error when `dry_run=false`

title: Add rate limiting to authentication and write endpoints
body:
## Summary

Apply rate limiting to login and sensitive write endpoints to prevent brute-force and abuse.

## Acceptance criteria

- `POST /api/v1/auth/login` — max 10 attempts per IP per minute; returns 429 with `Retry-After` header
- All `POST/PUT/DELETE` endpoints — max 200 requests per IP per minute
- Rate limits are in-memory (no external dependency)
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining` and `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers on all rate-limited responses

## Implementation notes

- Use `golang.org/x/time/rate` token bucket
- Keyed by real IP (use `middleware.RealIP` from chi)

title: Add webhook events for rule and config changes
body:
## Summary

Allow external systems to subscribe to events when rules or configuration change.

## Acceptance criteria

- `POST /api/v1/webhooks` — register a webhook URL and event types
- Events: `rule.created`, `rule.updated`, `rule.deleted`, `config.imported`, `source.refreshed`
- Payload: `{event, resource_type, resource_id, timestamp, actor}`
- Delivery with 3 retries and exponential backoff
- Delivery log viewable in the UI

## Implementation notes

- Use a background worker goroutine with a channel-based queue
- Sign payloads with HMAC-SHA256 using a webhook secret

title: Add Prometheus metrics endpoint
body:
## Summary

Expose an `/metrics` endpoint in Prometheus exposition format for monitoring integration.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /metrics` returns Prometheus text format
- Metrics include: `openfiltr_queries_total{action}`, `openfiltr_query_duration_ms`, `openfiltr_blocked_domains_total`, `openfiltr_cache_hit_total`, `openfiltr_upstream_errors_total{upstream}`
- Endpoint can be protected with a separate token or IP allowlist

## Implementation notes

- Use `github.com/prometheus/client_golang`
- Register collectors at startup

title: [EPIC] Authentication & Security
body:
## Epic: Authentication & Security

Ensures OpenFiltr is secure by default with authentication, authorisation, CSRF protection, and audit logging.

### Child issues

- Implement JWT authentication with configurable expiry
- Add CSRF protection for browser sessions
- Implement audit logging for all destructive actions
- Add role-based access control (v1.1)
- Generate SBOM on every release

### Acceptance criteria

- No unauthenticated access to protected endpoints
- All destructive actions logged with actor, timestamp, and resource ID
- Vulnerabilities reported via GitHub Security Advisories only

title: Implement JWT authentication with configurable expiry
body:
## Summary

Issue short-lived JWT tokens on login and validate them on every protected request.

## Acceptance criteria

- JWT is signed with `HS256` using a configurable secret
- Token expiry is configurable (default 24 hours)
- Token claims include `user_id`, `username`, `role`, `exp`, `iat`
- Token validated from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header or `openfiltr_token` cookie
- Expired tokens return 401

## Security notes

- JWT secret must be at least 32 characters
- Warn at startup if secret is the default placeholder value

title: Add CSRF protection for browser sessions
body:
## Summary

Protect cookie-based browser sessions against cross-site request forgery.

## Acceptance criteria

- CSRF token is set in a response header on login (`X-CSRF-Token`)
- All state-changing requests from browser sessions require `X-CSRF-Token` header
- API token authentication (Bearer) bypasses CSRF check
- CSRF validation failure returns 403

## Implementation notes

- Use the synchroniser token pattern
- Store CSRF token in a separate `SameSite=Strict` cookie

title: Implement audit logging for all destructive actions
body:
## Summary

Record every create, update, and delete operation in the `audit_events` table with actor and context.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE` request logged with: `user_id`, `action`, `resource_type`, `resource_id`, `ip_address`, `created_at`
- `GET /api/v1/audit` returns paginated audit events (authenticated)
- Audit log is append-only — no delete endpoint
- Failed authentication attempts are also logged

## Implementation notes

- Call `logAudit(r, action, resourceType, resourceID)` helper from each handler
- Use background goroutine to avoid blocking the request

title: Add role-based access control (admin, viewer, operator)
body:
## Summary

Introduce multiple user roles with different permission levels.

## Roles

| Role | Permissions |
|------|-------------|
| admin | Full access |
| operator | Read + write rules; no user management |
| viewer | Read-only |

## Acceptance criteria

- `POST /api/v1/users` (admin only) — create users with roles
- Role is stored in JWT claims
- Middleware enforces role requirements per route
- Insufficient permissions return 403

## Implementation notes

- Define route-level role requirements in the router

title: Generate SBOM on every release
body:
## Summary

Automatically generate a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) as part of the release workflow.

## Motivation

SBOMs enable users and security teams to audit the components included in each release.

## Acceptance criteria

- SBOM is generated in CycloneDX JSON format
- SBOM is attached to every GitHub Release
- SBOM covers both Go dependencies and Node.js dependencies
- Generation is automated in the release GitHub Actions workflow

## Implementation notes

- Use `cyclonedx-gomod` for Go
- Use `@cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm` for Node.js

title: [EPIC] React Frontend UI
body:
## Epic: React Frontend UI

A responsive, accessible web UI built with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS.

### Pages

- Dashboard — stats cards, activity chart, recent blocked domains
- Filtering — block rules, allow rules, rule sources
- DNS — upstream servers, local entries
- Clients — client list, groups
- Activity Log — query log with filters and live updates
- Settings — API tokens, import/export, system info
- Login / First-run Setup

### Design principles

- Mobile-first, responsive layout
- Dark mode enabled by default
- Accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- No unnecessary modals — inline editing where possible
- Keyboard shortcuts for power users

title: Set up Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS project
body:
## Summary

Scaffold the React frontend with Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and essential libraries.

## Acceptance criteria

- `web/` directory contains a Vite + React + TypeScript project
- Tailwind CSS configured with a custom OpenFiltr theme
- `npm run dev` starts a development server that proxies API calls to `localhost:3000`
- `npm run build` produces a `web/dist/` directory
- ESLint and Prettier configured
- Path aliases set up (`@/` → `web/src/`)

## Dependencies

- `vite`, `react`, `react-dom`, `typescript`
- `tailwindcss`, `@headlessui/react`
- `react-router-dom` (v6)
- `@tanstack/react-query` (data fetching)
- `recharts` (charts)
- `lucide-react` (icons)

title: Build layout with sidebar navigation and dark mode
body:
## Summary

Create the application shell with a collapsible sidebar, top bar, and dark mode support.

## Acceptance criteria

- Sidebar with links to: Dashboard, Filtering, DNS, Clients, Activity, Settings
- Active route highlighted in sidebar
- Dark mode enabled by default; toggle stored in `localStorage`
- Sidebar collapses to icon-only mode on narrow viewports
- Top bar shows page title, user avatar/logout button
- Layout is responsive down to 375px width

## Implementation notes

- Use Tailwind `dark:` variants
- Sidebar state managed with `useState` + `localStorage`

title: Build dashboard page with stats cards and activity chart
body:
## Summary

Create the main dashboard showing key metrics and recent DNS activity.

## Acceptance criteria

- Stats cards: Total Queries, Blocked Queries, Block Rate %, Active Rules
- Bar chart of queries per hour (last 24 hours)
- Table of top 10 blocked domains
- Recent activity feed (last 20 queries, live updates every 10s)
- All data from `/api/v1/system/stats` and `/api/v1/activity`
- Skeleton loading states shown while data fetches

## Implementation notes

- Use `recharts` `BarChart` for the activity chart
- Use `@tanstack/react-query` with `refetchInterval: 10000` for live updates

title: Build filtering management UI (block/allow rules, sources)
body:
## Summary

UI pages for managing block rules, allow rules, and external rule sources.

## Acceptance criteria

- Tabbed interface: Block Rules | Allow Rules | Sources
- Each tab shows a searchable, sortable table with pagination
- Inline add-rule form at the top of each table
- Edit rule in-place (no modal)
- Delete with confirmation
- Sources tab shows `rule_count`, `last_updated_at`, and a Refresh button
- Empty states with helpful copy

## Implementation notes

- Use `@tanstack/react-query` mutations for create/update/delete
- Optimistic updates for toggle-enabled switch

title: Build DNS management UI (upstreams, local entries)
body:
## Summary

UI pages for managing upstream DNS servers and local DNS entries.

## Acceptance criteria

- Two sections: Upstream Servers | Local DNS Entries
- Upstream servers table with priority ordering (drag-to-reorder)
- Local entries table with type badge (A/AAAA/CNAME etc), host, value, TTL
- Add/edit/delete for both
- Protocol badge on upstream servers (UDP/TCP/DoH/DoT)

## Implementation notes

- Use `@dnd-kit/sortable` for upstream drag-to-reorder
- Entry type badge with distinct colour per type

title: Build clients and groups management UI
body:
## Summary

UI for registering network clients and organising them into groups.

## Acceptance criteria

- Clients table: name, identifier, type badge (IP/CIDR/MAC), group badge, edit/delete
- Groups list: name, client count, edit/delete
- Client registration form: name, identifier, identifier type, group dropdown
- Assign client to group inline
- Unassigned clients shown in a default group

## Implementation notes

- Group dropdown populated from `/api/v1/groups`
- Validate IP/CIDR format client-side before submit

title: Build activity log page with live updates and filters
body:
## Summary

A live-updating, filterable view of DNS query activity.

## Acceptance criteria

- Table columns: Time, Client IP, Domain, Type, Action (allowed/blocked)
- Action column: green badge for allowed, red for blocked
- Filters: client IP, domain (substring), action, date range
- Auto-refresh every 5 seconds (can be paused)
- Click a domain to add a block/allow rule directly
- Export to CSV

## Implementation notes

- Virtualise the table for large result sets (`@tanstack/virtual`)
- Pause auto-refresh when user has active filters to avoid jarring updates

title: Build settings page (API tokens, import/export, system info)
body:
## Summary

Settings page covering API tokens management, configuration import/export, and system information.

## Acceptance criteria

- **API Tokens** section: list tokens, create (show raw token once with copy button), revoke
- **Export** button: downloads current config as YAML
- **Import** section: drag-and-drop or file-picker YAML upload; shows diff preview before confirming
- **System Info** section: version, uptime, DB size, upstream status

## Implementation notes

- Use `URL.createObjectURL` for the YAML download
- Show a diff of import changes using a simple two-column view

title: Build login page and first-run setup wizard
body:
## Summary

Create the login page and a first-run setup wizard for new installations.

## Acceptance criteria

- Login page: username + password form, error message on failure, redirect to dashboard on success
- First-run setup: detected via `GET /api/v1/auth/setup` returning 200; wizard collects username + password (with strength indicator)
- Redirect to setup wizard if no users exist
- Both pages work correctly on mobile

## Implementation notes

- Use `react-router-dom` for auth-protected routes
- Store JWT in `localStorage` and `openfiltr_token` cookie
- Password strength: require 8+ chars, show strength bar

title: Ensure mobile-responsive layout (WCAG 2.1 AA)
body:
## Summary

Audit and fix all pages for mobile responsiveness and WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility.

## Acceptance criteria

- All pages usable on 375px wide viewport (iPhone SE)
- Keyboard navigation works on all interactive elements
- Focus indicators visible
- All images and icons have `aria-label` or `alt` text
- Colour contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text, ≥ 3:1 for large text
- No horizontal scroll on any page at 375px

## Implementation notes

- Test with Lighthouse accessibility audit (score ≥ 90)
- Use `axe-core` in tests

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for common actions
body:
## Summary

Add keyboard shortcuts to speed up common operations for power users.

## Acceptance criteria

- `?` — opens keyboard shortcut help overlay
- `n` — opens new rule/entry form on the current page
- `g d` — go to Dashboard
- `g f` — go to Filtering
- `g a` — go to Activity
- `g s` — go to Settings
- `/` — focus the search/filter input
- Shortcuts are disabled when focus is in an input field

## Implementation notes

- Use a custom `useKeyboard` hook
- Display shortcuts in a `<kbd>` styled badge in the UI

title: Add live domain test tool to the UI
body:
## Summary

Allow users to test whether a specific domain would be blocked or allowed without making a real DNS query.

## Acceptance criteria

- Input field accepting a domain name
- Shows result: blocked/allowed with the matching rule (if any)
- Available from the Filtering page or as a global search-bar action
- Keyboard shortcut: `t` to open the test tool

## Implementation notes

- Add `GET /api/v1/filtering/test?domain=example.com` endpoint on the backend
- Returns: `{action: "blocked"|"allowed", rule: {id, pattern, rule_type} | null}`

title: Add import preview diff view
body:
## Summary

When importing a YAML configuration, show a visual diff of what will change before the user confirms.

## Acceptance criteria

- After selecting an import file, call `POST /api/v1/config/import?dry_run=true`
- Display additions (green), removals (red), and unchanged items (grey)
- Counts: `{created: N, updated: N, deleted: N}`
- Confirm button is only enabled after viewing the diff

## Implementation notes

- Group diff by section (block_rules, allow_rules, etc.)
- Only show changed sections; collapse unchanged ones

title: [EPIC] Configuration & Portability
body:
## Epic: Configuration & Portability

Make OpenFiltr configuration fully portable via YAML import/export.

### Child issues

- Implement full YAML config export
- Implement YAML config import with dry-run validation
- Add schema version field to all exported YAML
- Implement backup and restore via the API

### Acceptance criteria

- Full round-trip: export from instance A, import to instance B → identical configuration
- Exported YAML can be committed to git and used as infrastructure-as-code

title: Add schema version field to all exported YAML files
body:
## Summary

Add a `version` field to all exported YAML files to enable forward-compatible imports.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every exported YAML file includes `version: 1` at the top
- Import endpoint validates the version field and rejects unsupported versions with a clear error message
- Version field is documented in the configuration reference

## Implementation notes

- This is a `good-first-issue` — it just adds a field to the export YAML struct and validates it on import

title: Implement backup and restore via the API
body:
## Summary

Add dedicated backup and restore endpoints that capture the full database state.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/backup` — returns a complete YAML backup including all user data
- `POST /api/v1/restore` — accepts a backup file and restores all data (destructive)
- Restore requires admin role and confirms with a `confirm=true` query parameter
- Backup file includes `created_at` and instance `base_url`

## Implementation notes

- Use SQLite `VACUUM INTO` for a clean binary backup option (future enhancement)
- YAML backup is human-readable and preferred for portability

title: [EPIC] Deployment & Installation
body:
## Epic: Deployment & Installation

Make OpenFiltr trivially easy to install and run in any environment.

### Child issues

- Write curl install script
- Create systemd service unit
- Publish Docker image to GHCR on release
- Support arm64 / Raspberry Pi builds
- Add container healthcheck

### Acceptance criteria

- Fresh Ubuntu 22.04 install: `curl install.sh | sh` completes in < 60 seconds
- Raspberry Pi 4 (arm64): Docker image works
- All deployment methods covered in documentation

title: Write curl install script with dry-run and non-root support
body:
## Summary

Create `scripts/install.sh` — a bash installer that sets up OpenFiltr on Linux with a single command.

## Acceptance criteria

- Detects OS (linux) and architecture (amd64, arm64, armv7)
- Downloads the correct binary from GitHub Releases
- `--dry-run` flag prints actions without executing them
- `--version <tag>` flag installs a specific version
- Creates `openfiltr` system user (or `--no-root` to install for current user)
- Installs binary to `/usr/local/bin/openfiltr`
- Creates `/etc/openfiltr/` config directory
- Writes and enables a systemd unit
- Prints success message with the URL to open

## Implementation notes

- Use `curl -fsSL` for downloads
- Verify SHA-256 checksum before installing
- Support both `root` and `sudo` invocations

title: Create systemd service unit for OpenFiltr
body:
## Summary

Write a systemd service unit that runs OpenFiltr as a non-root system service.

## Acceptance criteria

- Unit file at `deploy/systemd/openfiltr.service`
- Runs as the `openfiltr` system user
- `Restart=on-failure` with `RestartSec=5`
- `CapabilityBoundingSet=CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE` (to bind port 53 without root)
- `AmbientCapabilities=CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE`
- Standard output logged to journald
- `WantedBy=multi-user.target`

## Implementation notes

- Use `ProtectSystem=strict` and `PrivateTmp=true` for sandboxing

title: Publish Docker image to GHCR on release
body:
## Summary

Automate Docker image builds and pushes to GitHub Container Registry on every tagged release.

## Acceptance criteria

- `docker pull ghcr.io/openfiltr/openfiltr:latest` works after a release
- Tags: `latest`, `v{major}`, `v{major}.{minor}`, `v{major}.{minor}.{patch}`
- Multi-platform build: `linux/amd64`, `linux/arm64`
- Image includes SBOM label
- Release workflow pushes image before attaching binaries to the release

## Implementation notes

- Use `docker/build-push-action@v6` with `platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64`
- Use `docker/metadata-action@v5` for tag generation

title: Support arm64 / Raspberry Pi builds in CI
body:
## Summary

Ensure cross-compiled arm64 binaries are produced and tested in CI.

## Acceptance criteria

- `linux/arm64` binary produced in CI and attached to releases
- Docker image built for `linux/arm64` using QEMU emulation
- `armv7` (32-bit Raspberry Pi) binary also produced

## Implementation notes

- Use `docker/setup-qemu-action@v3` for cross-compilation in Docker
- CGO cross-compilation requires `CC=aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc`
- Add `linux/arm/v7` to Docker platforms

title: Add container healthcheck to Docker image
body:
## Summary

Add a `HEALTHCHECK` instruction to the Dockerfile that verifies the HTTP API is responding.

## Acceptance criteria

- `HEALTHCHECK` calls `wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/api/v1/system/health`
- `--interval=30s`, `--timeout=10s`, `--start-period=10s`, `--retries=3`
- `docker ps` shows `(healthy)` after startup
- `docker-compose.yml` also includes a `healthcheck` section

## Implementation notes

- This is a `good-first-issue` — a one-line addition to the Dockerfile

title: [EPIC] Documentation & OpenAPI
body:
## Epic: Documentation & OpenAPI

Comprehensive documentation so any developer or operator can get started without asking questions.

### Child issues

- Write comprehensive OpenAPI 3.1 specification
- Write installation guide
- Write configuration reference
- Write API authentication guide
- Set up generated API docs site from OpenAPI spec (v1.1)

### Acceptance criteria

- Any developer can run OpenFiltr locally in under 5 minutes using only the README
- All 50+ API endpoints are documented in the OpenAPI spec
- No undocumented configuration options

title: Write comprehensive OpenAPI 3.1 specification
body:
## Summary

Document all API endpoints in an OpenAPI 3.1 YAML file served at `/openapi.yaml`.

## Acceptance criteria

- All endpoints documented with request/response schemas
- All schemas use `$ref` to named components
- Includes examples for common operations
- Authentication documented (BearerAuth scheme)
- Served live from the server at `/openapi.yaml`
- Passes `spectral lint` with zero errors

## Implementation notes

- File at `openapi/openapi.yaml`
- Keep in sync with handler implementations
- Add lint check to CI

title: Write installation guide (curl, Docker, manual binary)
body:
## Summary

Create detailed installation documentation covering all supported methods.

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/installation.md` covers: curl one-liner, Docker, Docker Compose, manual binary installation
- Each method includes prerequisites and post-install verification steps
- Raspberry Pi / ARM64 section
- Troubleshooting section for common errors (port 53 in use, firewall rules)

## Implementation notes

- Link from README quick-install section to docs
- Include DNS configuration examples for common routers

title: Write configuration reference documentation
body:
## Summary

Document every configuration option with type, default value, and description.

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/configuration.md` documents every field in `app.yaml`
- Each option includes: YAML key, type, default, env var override, description
- Includes full annotated example configuration
- Documents all environment variables

## Implementation notes

- This is a `good-first-issue` — mostly writing, no code changes needed

title: Write API authentication guide
body:
## Summary

Document how to authenticate with the OpenFiltr API using both JWT and API tokens.

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/api-auth.md` documents: login flow, JWT usage, API token creation, token revocation
- Includes `curl` examples for each authentication method
- Documents cookie-based authentication for browser sessions
- Explains when to use JWT vs API tokens

## Implementation notes

- This is a `good-first-issue` suitable for technical writers

title: Set up generated API docs site from OpenAPI spec
body:
## Summary

Deploy a rendered API documentation site (e.g. Redoc or Stoplight Elements) from the OpenAPI spec.

## Acceptance criteria

- API docs site published to GitHub Pages on every `main` push
- Uses Redoc or Stoplight Elements
- Custom OpenFiltr branding
- Links to docs from README badge

## Implementation notes

- Deploy from `gh-pages` branch using GitHub Actions
- No external hosting required

title: [EPIC] Community & Governance Setup
body:
## Epic: Community & Governance Setup

Establish all the community infrastructure needed for open-source contributions.

### Child issues

- Create issue templates (bug, feature, RFC)
- Write GOVERNANCE.md and RFC process
- Set up repository labels and milestone structure
- Publish initial public roadmap
- Create SECURITY.md and vulnerability disclosure policy

### Acceptance criteria

- New contributors can find everything they need in CONTRIBUTING.md
- Issues are consistently labelled
- Security vulnerabilities have a clear private disclosure path

title: Create issue templates (bug report, feature request, RFC)
body:
## Summary

Add GitHub issue form templates to guide contributors in filing well-structured reports.

## Acceptance criteria

- Bug report template: description, steps to reproduce, expected/actual behaviour, version, install method
- Feature request template: summary, motivation, proposed solution, alternatives
- RFC template: summary, motivation, detailed design, drawbacks, alternatives, unresolved questions
- All templates use GitHub's YAML issue form syntax
- Labels auto-applied: `bug triage`, `enhancement triage`, `rfc discussion`

## Implementation notes

- Files in `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`
- This is a `good-first-issue`

title: Write GOVERNANCE.md and RFC process documentation
body:
## Summary

Document how decisions are made in the OpenFiltr project.

## Acceptance criteria

- `GOVERNANCE.md` covers: project vision, lazy consensus model, RFC process (14-day comment period), maintainer lifecycle, conflict resolution
- RFC process has its own section and links to the RFC issue template
- Document is < 500 words — concise and actionable

## Implementation notes

- Model on other successful OSS projects (Kubernetes, Rust, etc.)
- Link from CONTRIBUTING.md and README

title: Set up repository labels and milestone structure
body:
## Summary

Create the full set of GitHub labels and milestones needed to organise the roadmap.

## Acceptance criteria

- Labels defined in `.github/project-setup/labels.json`
- Labels created: `epic`, `good-first-issue`, `AI-AGENT-COMPATIBLE`, `v1.0`, `v1.1`, `v1.2`, `backend`, `frontend`, `dns`, `api`, `security`, `config`, `infrastructure`, `documentation`, `community`, `performance`, `bug`, `enhancement`, `triage`, `rfc`, `needs-design`, `blocked`
- Milestones: `v1.0`, `v1.1`, `v1.2`
- `scripts/create-issues.sh` creates all labels and issues via `gh` CLI
- `create-project-issues.yml` workflow allows one-click seeding

## Implementation notes

- This is a `good-first-issue` and `AI-AGENT-COMPATIBLE`

title: Publish initial public roadmap
body:
## Summary

Create and maintain a public ROADMAP.md that clearly communicates what is planned and when.

## Acceptance criteria

- `ROADMAP.md` lists features for v1.0, v1.1, v1.2 with status indicators
- Roadmap is linked from the README
- Process for influencing the roadmap (RFC) is documented
- Roadmap is updated with each release

## Implementation notes

- Use checkboxes for status (checked = complete)
- Keep it brief — detailed specs belong in issues

title: Create SECURITY.md and vulnerability disclosure policy
body:
## Summary

Document how to report security vulnerabilities and what users can expect in response.

## Acceptance criteria

- `SECURITY.md` covers: supported versions, private disclosure via GitHub Security Advisories, response timeline (48h ack, 5 day triage, 90 day fix)
- No public issue for vulnerabilities — redirect to private advisory
- Security baseline documented: CSRF, rate limiting, HttpOnly cookies, audit log, signed releases, SBOM

## Implementation notes

- This is a `good-first-issue` — writing only

title: Write curl install script (scripts/install.sh)
body:
## Summary

Create `scripts/install.sh` — a production-ready bash installer for Linux and Raspberry Pi.

## Acceptance criteria

- Detects OS (`linux`) and architecture (`amd64`, `arm64`, `armv7`)
- Downloads binary from GitHub Releases with SHA-256 verification
- `--dry-run` prints actions without executing
- `--version <tag>` installs a specific release
- `--no-root` installs to `~/.local/bin` and skips systemd setup
- Creates `openfiltr` system user
- Writes binary to `/usr/local/bin/openfiltr`
- Creates `/etc/openfiltr/` config dir with default `app.yaml`
- Writes, enables and starts a systemd unit
- Prints coloured success message with the URL

## Implementation notes

- `set -euo pipefail` at the top
- Use `curl -fsSL` with a timeout
- Verify checksum with `sha256sum -c`
